"A brother offended is harder to be won than a strong city." It is so easy to offend someone-and so dangerous! The Apostle Paul, knowing how a thoughtless action or comment could affect a member's attitude about the Church, urged us to give "no offence in any thing, that the ministry be not blamed" (2 Cor. 6:3). (Denise Turner, "If Any Man Offend Not," Ensign, Aug. 1998, 46)

tern after the wickedness that is creeping into our midst. Come out from among them and be ye separate, and touch not the unclean thing! Be ye clean that bear the vessels of the Lord. It is only by the practice of righteousness and personal purity, that we will be made fit to dwell in the presence of the Lord. A doctrine contrary to this is the worst kind of delusion. May God help us to live the life of a Saint and finally save us in his kingdom, is my prayer, in the name of Jesus Christ. Amen.
